headline: Typhoon Bavi Batters Eastern China and Taiwan as 1.8 Million Evacuated, While a Deadly Spanish Wildfire and a Sprawling US Heat Dome Mark a Day of Compounding Global Disasters
overview: Monday, July 13 brings a multi-front global natural disaster picture: Typhoon Bavi has made landfall on eastern China after prompting the evacuation of over 1.8 million people and injuring at least 134 in Taiwan, representing a major escalation from last week's Tropical Storm Maysak. Simultaneously, the US heat dome intensifies its reach southward into Southern California — where an Extreme Heat Watch covering Los Angeles is now in effect through Thursday — and eastward toward the Philadelphia and Michigan corridors, while new Red Flag fire weather warnings ignite across northern Minnesota and the Great Plains. A fast-moving wildfire in southern Spain's Almeria region has killed at least 12 people, marking one of the country's deadliest fire events in years.

events.title: Typhoon Bavi Slams Eastern China and Taiwan — 1.8 Million Evacuated
events.location: Zhejiang and Fujian Provinces, China; Taiwan
events.summary: Typhoon Bavi made landfall on China's eastern coast, battering Wenzhou and surrounding areas in Zhejiang Province with damaging winds and flooding rains after China evacuated over 1.8 million people in anticipation of the storm. Taiwan reported at least 134 people injured as the typhoon passed nearby, while CNBC reports the storm threatens days of continued heavy rain across eastern China. The system represents a significant escalation from Tropical Storm Maysak, which struck southern China just days earlier, and comes as scientists warn of an increasingly active western Pacific storm season.

articles.title: Typhoon Bavi batters east China, Taiwan reports 134 injured
Typhoon Bavi batters eastern China, threatens days of heavy rain

events.title: US Heat Dome Spreads South and East — Los Angeles Under Extreme Heat Watch Through Thursday
events.location: Southern California, Northern Plains, Rockies, Upper Midwest, Mid-Atlantic — USA
events.summary: The multi-day US heat dome has significantly expanded its footprint, with NWS Los Angeles issuing a new Extreme Heat Watch for the greater Los Angeles area — including Downtown LA, the San Fernando Valley, and surrounding counties — with temperatures of 95–110°F forecast Tuesday through Thursday. Extreme Heat Warnings remain in effect across Montana, Wyoming, Colorado, Idaho, Utah, North Dakota, Minnesota, Wisconsin, and Michigan, with heat index values up to 110°F, while a new Extreme Heat Watch covering Philadelphia, Delaware, and southern New Jersey has been issued for Wednesday. The Weather Channel reports the heat wave is on track to smash all-time records in the Rockies before continuing its eastward march through the week.

articles.title: Heat wave could threaten all-time record highs in Rockies

events.title: Southern Spain Wildfire Kills at Least 12 Near Almeria — One of Country's Deadliest
events.summary: A fast-moving wildfire near Los Gallardos in the Almeria province of southern Spain has killed at least 12 people and left 19 others missing, making it one of the deadliest wildfires in Spain's modern history. Hundreds of firefighters have been deployed to combat the blaze, which erupted amid the ongoing Western European heatwave that has been pushing temperatures well above seasonal norms across the Iberian Peninsula. The Washington Post reports the fire is being driven by extreme heat and dry conditions consistent with the broader European heat event.

articles.title: Hundreds of firefighters battle wildfire in southern Spain that killed at least 12
One of Spain's deadliest wildfires has killed at least 11 people, with 19 others missing

events.title: US Multi-State Flash Flood Crisis Continues — New Warnings Issued Across Appalachians, Texas, and Midwest Rivers
events.location: North Carolina, Virginia, Tennessee, Texas (Dallas), Illinois, Missouri, Iowa — USA
events.summary: Active Flash Flood Warnings and Flood Watches continue across a broad swath of the eastern and central US on July 13, with new overnight warnings issued for North Carolina's Blue Ridge foothills, the Virginia–North Carolina border region, and Dallas-Fort Worth, where 2–5 inches of rain fell in hours. Extended Flood Warnings remain active along the Illinois River through July 17–19, and a new Flood Watch has been issued for the Cedar River in Iowa through midweek. FOX Weather reports rounds of heavy rain continue to target the Heartland and Gulf Coast with no near-term end in sight.

events.title: M6.4 Earthquake Strikes Near Papua New Guinea — Near Ongoing Submarine Volcanic Site
events.location: 191 km SE of Lorengau, Papua New Guinea, Southwest Pacific
events.summary: A magnitude 6.4 earthquake struck at shallow depth (10 km) approximately 191 km southeast of Lorengau, Papua New Guinea at 08:53 UTC on July 13, according to USGS data. The USGS assigned a green alert level and no tsunami alert was triggered, suggesting limited immediate threat to populated areas. Notably, the event occurs in the same general southwest Pacific region as the ongoing submarine volcanic eruption north of Papua New Guinea that has been tracked by NASA satellites since early July, though USGS data does not directly link the two events.

events.title: Red Flag Fire Weather Warnings Spread Across Northern Minnesota and Great Plains
events.location: Northern Minnesota, Nebraska Panhandle, South Dakota, USA
events.summary: NWS offices in Duluth, Grand Forks, Cheyenne, and Rapid City issued multiple Red Flag Warnings and Fire Weather Watches across northern Minnesota, the Nebraska Panhandle, and western South Dakota on July 13, driven by gusty southwest winds of 10–35 mph, relative humidity as low as 10–25%, and critically dry fuels baked by the ongoing heat dome. The warnings cover vast stretches of the Superior National Forest, the Boundary Waters Canoe Area Wilderness, the Pine Ridge area, and Nebraska's national grasslands — ecologically sensitive regions where any ignition could escalate rapidly. The Red Flag conditions are expected to persist through Wednesday in some areas.

events.title: Western Europe Heatwave Persists — Paris Holds at 35°C, Spain Wildfire Conditions Critical
events.location: Paris, France; London, United Kingdom; Berlin, Germany; Almeria, Spain — Western Europe
events.summary: Current weather data confirms the Western European heatwave remains firmly established on July 13, with Paris recording a maximum of 35°C, London 27°C, and Berlin 28°C — all significantly above seasonal norms for mid-July. The heat is directly linked to the deadly Almeria wildfire in southern Spain and ongoing fire emergencies in Portugal and Greece reported in recent days. With no significant cool front forecast in the near term, public health authorities across France, the UK, and Germany have maintained heat-health advisories for vulnerable populations.

articles.title: Hundreds of firefighters battle wildfire in southern Spain that killed at least 12
